```
from app.services.booking_service import (
    get_sessions_for_date, check_booking_conflicts, create_reservation,
    cancel_reservation, reschedule_reservation, join_waitlist, promote_waitlist,
    get_user_bookings, leave_waitlist
)

def test_get_sessions_for_date_returns_sessions(app, db, sample_session):
    """Verify sessions are returned for the correct date in correct format."""
    with app.app_context():
        date_str = sample_session.start_time.strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
        result = get_sessions_for_date(date_str)
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0]["title"] == "Morning Yoga"
        assert result[0]["spots_remaining"] == 15
        assert "AM" in result[0]["start_time"] or "PM" in result[0]["start_time"]

def test_get_sessions_invalid_date_raises(app):
    """Verify ValueError raised for bad date format."""
    with app.app_context():
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Invalid date format"):
            get_sessions_for_date("2026-03-31")  # wrong format

def test_create_reservation_success(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """Verify successful booking creates a confirmed reservation."""
    with app.app_context():
        result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        assert "reservation_id" in result
        reservation = Reservation.query.get(result["reservation_id"])
        assert reservation.status == "confirmed"
        assert reservation.user_id == sample_users["customer"].id

def test_create_reservation_duplicate_blocked(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """Verify user cannot double-book the same session."""
    with app.app_context():
        create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "already have a reservation" in result["reason"]

def test_conflict_detection(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Verify overlapping session times are detected."""
    with app.app_context():
        tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
        start1 = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
        session1 = StudioSession(title="Yoga", instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id, room_id=sample_room.id, start_time=start1, end_time=start1 + timedelta(hours=1), capacity=15, is_active=True)
        session2 = StudioSession(title="Pilates", instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id, room_id=sample_room.id, start_time=start1 + timedelta(minutes=30), end_time=start1 + timedelta(hours=1, minutes=30), capacity=15, is_active=True)
        db.session.add_all([session1, session2])
        db.session.commit()

        create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, session1.id)
        result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, session2.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["action"] == "conflict"

def test_capacity_full_offers_waitlist(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Verify full session returns waitlist option."""
    with app.app_context():
        tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
        start = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
        session = StudioSession(title="Full Class", instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id, room_id=sample_room.id, start_time=start, end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1), capacity=1, is_active=True)
        db.session.add(session)
        db.session.commit()

        create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, session.id)  # fills the 1 spot

        other_user = User(username="other", email="other@test.com", role="customer", credit_score=100)
        other_user.set_password("TestPass123!")
        db.session.add(other_user)
        db.session.commit()

        result = create_reservation(other_user.id, session.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["action"] == "waitlist"

def test_cancel_before_12h_no_breach(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """Cancel >12 hours before start: no breach flag."""
    with app.app_context():
        create_result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        result = cancel_reservation(create_result["reservation_id"], sample_users["customer"].id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        assert result["breach"] is False
        reservation = Reservation.query.get(create_result["reservation_id"])
        assert reservation.status == "canceled"
        assert reservation.breach_flag is False

def test_cancel_within_12h_breach(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Cancel <12 hours before start: breach flag set, credit deducted."""
    with app.app_context():
        start = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(hours=6)  # 6 hours from now
        session = StudioSession(title="Soon", instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id, room_id=sample_room.id, start_time=start, end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1), capacity=15, is_active=True)
        db.session.add(session)
        db.session.commit()

        create_result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, session.id)
        result = cancel_reservation(create_result["reservation_id"], sample_users["customer"].id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        assert result["breach"] is True

        from app.models.analytics import CreditHistory
        credit = CreditHistory.query.filter_by(user_id=sample_users["customer"].id, event_type="late_cancel").first()
        assert credit is not None
        assert credit.points == -1

def test_waitlist_promotion_on_cancel(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """When a reservation is canceled, waitlist #1 gets promoted."""
    with app.app_context():
        tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
        start = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
        session = StudioSession(title="Full", instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id, room_id=sample_room.id, start_time=start, end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1), capacity=1, is_active=True)
        db.session.add(session)
        db.session.commit()

        create_result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, session.id)

        waitlister = User(username="waiter", email="wait@test.com", role="customer", credit_score=100)
        waitlister.set_password("TestPass123!")
        db.session.add(waitlister)
        db.session.commit()

        join_waitlist(waitlister.id, session.id)
        cancel_reservation(create_result["reservation_id"], sample_users["customer"].id)

        promoted = Reservation.query.filter_by(user_id=waitlister.id, session_id=session.id, status="confirmed").first()
        assert promoted is not None

def test_reschedule_creates_linked_reservation(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Reschedule cancels old, creates new, and links them."""
    with app.app_context():
        tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
        start1 = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
        start2 = tomorrow.replace(hour=14, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
        session1 = StudioSession(title="AM", instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id, room_id=sample_room.id, start_time=start1, end_time=start1 + timedelta(hours=1), capacity=15, is_active=True)
        session2 = StudioSession(title="PM", instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id, room_id=sample_room.id, start_time=start2, end_time=start2 + timedelta(hours=1), capacity=15, is_active=True)
        db.session.add_all([session1, session2])
        db.session.commit()

        create_result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, session1.id)
        result = reschedule_reservation(create_result["reservation_id"], session2.id, sample_users["customer"].id)

        assert result["success"] is True
        old = Reservation.query.get(create_result["reservation_id"])
        assert old.status == "rescheduled"
        new = Reservation.query.get(result["new_reservation_id"])
        assert new.status == "confirmed"
        assert new.original_reservation_id == old.id

def test_low_credit_requires_approval(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """User with credit < 50 gets pending_approval status."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = sample_users["customer"]
        user.credit_score = 40
        db.session.commit()

        result = create_reservation(user.id, sample_session.id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        reservation = Reservation.query.get(result["reservation_id"])
        assert reservation.status == "pending_approval"

def test_very_low_credit_blocked(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """User with credit < 20 is blocked entirely."""
    with app.app_context():
        user = sample_users["customer"]
        user.credit_score = 15
        db.session.commit()

        result = create_reservation(user.id, sample_session.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["action"] == "blocked"
```

1	"""
2	Booking business logic layer for StudioOps.
3	
4	All route handlers should call these functions rather than touching the ORM
5	directly. Every function validates its inputs, raises descriptive errors where
6	appropriate, and returns a structured result dict so the caller can act on the
7	outcome without inspecting exceptions for control flow.
8	"""
9	import logging
10	from datetime import datetime, timedelta
11	
12	from sqlalchemy import and_, or_
13	
14	from app.extensions import db
15	from app.models.studio import StudioSession, Reservation, Waitlist, CheckIn
16	from app.models.analytics import CreditHistory
17	from app.models.user import User
18	
19	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
20	
21	
22	# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
23	
24	def _fmt_time(dt: datetime) -> str:
25	    """Format a datetime to 12-hour time string, e.g. '10:00 AM'."""
26	    return dt.strftime("%I:%M %p").lstrip("0") or "12:00 AM"
27	
28	
29	def _fmt_date(dt: datetime) -> str:
30	    """Format a datetime to MM/DD/YYYY string."""
31	    return dt.strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
32	
33	
34	def _fmt_datetime(dt: datetime) -> str:
35	    """Format a datetime to 'MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM AM/PM'."""
36	    return dt.strftime("%m/%d/%Y %I:%M %p")
37	
38	
39	def _reservation_dict(reservation: Reservation) -> dict:
40	    """
41	    Convert a Reservation ORM object to the standard booking dict used by
42	    get_user_bookings().
43	    """
44	    session = reservation.session
45	    instructor = session.instructor
46	    room = session.room
47	    now = datetime.utcnow()
48	
49	    hours_until = (session.start_time - now).total_seconds() / 3600
50	    can_act = reservation.status == "confirmed" and hours_until > 12
51	
52	    # Determine whether a review already exists for this reservation
53	    from app.models.review import Review
54	    existing_review = Review.query.filter_by(
55	        reservation_id=reservation.id,
56	        user_id=reservation.user_id,
57	    ).first()
58	    has_review = existing_review is not None
59	    can_review = reservation.status == "completed" and not has_review
60	
61	    return {
62	        "reservation_id": reservation.id,
63	        "session_id": session.id,
64	        "session_title": session.title,
65	        "session_date": _fmt_date(session.start_time),
66	        "session_start": _fmt_time(session.start_time),
67	        "session_end": _fmt_time(session.end_time),
68	        "instructor_name": instructor.username if instructor else "—",
69	        "room_name": room.name if room else "—",
70	        "status": reservation.status,
71	        "breach_flag": reservation.breach_flag,
72	        "booked_at": _fmt_datetime(reservation.created_at),
73	        "can_cancel": can_act,
74	        "can_reschedule": can_act,
75	        "can_review": can_review,
76	        "has_review": has_review,
77	    }
78	
79	
80	def reservation_to_dict(reservation: Reservation) -> dict:
81	    """Public wrapper so route handlers can convert an ORM Reservation to the
82	    standard booking dict used by ``booking_card.html``."""
83	    return _reservation_dict(reservation)
84	
85	
86	# ── Function 1 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
87	
88	def get_sessions_for_date(date_str: str, instructor_id: int = None) -> list[dict]:
89	    """
90	    Return all active studio sessions for a given date.
91	
92	    Parameters
93	    ----------
94	    date_str : str
95	        Date in MM/DD/YYYY format (e.g., "03/31/2026").
96	    instructor_id : int, optional
97	        When supplied, restrict results to sessions taught by this instructor.
98

1	from datetime import datetime
2	from flask_login import UserMixin
3	from ..extensions import db
4	
5	
6	class User(UserMixin, db.Model):
7	    __tablename__ = "users"
8	
9	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
10	    username = db.Column(db.String(80), unique=True, nullable=False)
11	    email = db.Column(db.String(120), unique=True, nullable=False)
12	    password_hash = db.Column(db.String(256), nullable=False)
13	    role = db.Column(db.String(20), nullable=False, default="customer")
14	    credit_score = db.Column(db.Integer, default=100)
15	    is_active = db.Column(db.Boolean, default=True)
16	    failed_login_attempts = db.Column(db.Integer, default=0)
17	    locked_until = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=True)
18	    last_login = db.Column(db.DateTime, nullable=True)
19	    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
20	    updated_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow)
21	
22	    # Relationships
23	    login_attempts = db.relationship("LoginAttempt", back_populates="user", lazy="dynamic")
24	    reservations = db.relationship("Reservation", back_populates="user", foreign_keys="Reservation.user_id", lazy="dynamic")
25	    waitlist_entries = db.relationship("Waitlist", back_populates="user", lazy="dynamic")
26	    check_ins_performed = db.relationship("CheckIn", back_populates="staff", foreign_keys="CheckIn.staff_id", lazy="dynamic")
27	    authored_content = db.relationship("Content", back_populates="author", foreign_keys="Content.author_id", lazy="dynamic")
28	    content_versions = db.relationship("ContentVersion", back_populates="created_by_user", foreign_keys="ContentVersion.created_by", lazy="dynamic")
29	    reviews = db.relationship("Review", back_populates="user", foreign_keys="Review.user_id", lazy="dynamic")
30	    filed_appeals = db.relationship("Appeal", back_populates="filer", foreign_keys="Appeal.user_id", lazy="dynamic")
31	    resolved_appeals = db.relationship("Appeal", back_populates="admin", foreign_keys="Appeal.admin_id", lazy="dynamic")
32	    analytics_events = db.relationship("AnalyticsEvent", back_populates="user", lazy="dynamic")
33	    credit_history = db.relationship("CreditHistory", back_populates="user", lazy="dynamic")
34	
35	    VALID_ROLES = ("customer", "staff", "editor", "admin")
36	
37	    def __repr__(self):
38	        return f"<User {self.username} [{self.role}]>"
39	
40	    def has_role(self, *roles: str) -> bool:
41	        return self.role in roles
42	
43	    # Flask-Login requires is_active as property; already a column, so override getter
44	    def get_id(self) -> str:
45	        return str(self.id)
46	
47	
48	class LoginAttempt(db.Model):
49	    __tablename__ = "login_attempts"
50	
51	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
52	    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
53	    ip_address = db.Column(db.String(45), nullable=True)
54	    success = db.Column(db.Boolean, nullable=False)
55	    attempted_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
56	
57	    user = db.relationship("User", back_populates="login_attempts")
58	
59	    def __repr__(self):
60	        result = "OK" if self.success else "FAIL"
61	        return f"<LoginAttempt user={self.user_id} {result} @ {self.attempted_at}>"
62

117	    logger.debug("get_sessions_for_date: date=%s instructor_id=%s", date_str, instructor_id)
118

133	        ).count()

135

143	            "end_time": _fmt_time(s.end_time),
144	            "date": _fmt_date(s.start_time),
145	            "capacity": s.capacity,
146	            "booked_count": booked_count,
147	            "spots_remaining": spots_remaining,
148	            "is_full": spots_remaining == 0,
149	        })
150	
151	    logger.info(

157	# ── Function 2 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
158	
159	def check_booking_conflicts(user_id: int, session_id: int) -> dict | None:
160	    """
161	    Detect whether booking *session_id* would overlap with an existing confirmed
162	    reservation held by *user_id*.
163	
164	    Parameters
165	    ----------
166	    user_id : int
167	    session_id : int
168	
169	    Returns
170	    -------
171	    None
172	        No conflict exists.
173	    dict
174	        ``{"conflict": True, "conflicting_session": {"id", "title",
175	        "start_time", "end_time"}}``
176	
177	    Raises
178	    ------
179	    ValueError
180	        If *session_id* is not found.
181	    """
182	    target = StudioSession.query.get(session_id)
183	    if target is None:
184	        raise ValueError("Session not found.")
185	
186	    confirmed = Reservation.query.filter_by(
187	        user_id=user_id, status="confirmed"
188	    ).all()
189	
190	    for res in confirmed:
191	        existing = res.session
192	        if existing.id == session_id:
193	            continue  # same session handled separately as duplicate check
194	        # Overlap: A starts before B ends AND B starts before A ends
195	        if existing.start_time < target.end_time and target.start_time < existing.end_time:
196	            logger.debug(
197	                "check_booking_conflicts: user %d conflicts session %d with %d",
198	                user_id, session_id, existing.id,
199	            )
200	            return {
201	                "conflict": True,
202	                "conflicting_session": {
203	                    "id": existing.id,
204	                    "title": existing.title,
205	                    "start_time": _fmt_time(existing.start_time),
206	                    "end_time": _fmt_time(existing.end_time),
207	                },
208	            }
209	
210	    return None
211

1	from datetime import datetime, timedelta
2	from typing import Optional, Tuple
3	from werkzeug.security import generate_password_hash, check_password_hash
4	from flask import current_app, request
5	from ..extensions import db
6	from ..models.user import User, LoginAttempt
7	
8	
9	def hash_password(password: str) -> str:
10	    return generate_password_hash(password)
11	
12	
13	def verify_password(password: str, password_hash: str) -> bool:
14	    return check_password_hash(password_hash, password)
15	
16	
17	def is_locked_out(user: User) -> bool:
18	    if user.locked_until and user.locked_until > datetime.utcnow():
19	        return True
20	    return False
21	
22	
23	def record_attempt(user: User, success: bool, ip: str = None) -> None:
24	    attempt = LoginAttempt(
25	        user_id=user.id,
26	        ip_address=ip or _get_client_ip(),
27	        success=success,
28	    )
29	    db.session.add(attempt)
30	
31	    if success:
32	        user.failed_login_attempts = 0
33	        user.locked_until = None
34	        user.last_login = datetime.utcnow()
35	    else:
36	        user.failed_login_attempts = (user.failed_login_attempts or 0) + 1
37	        max_attempts = current_app.config.get("MAX_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS", 5)
38	        if user.failed_login_attempts >= max_attempts:
39	            lockout_minutes = current_app.config.get("LOCKOUT_MINUTES", 15)
40	            user.locked_until = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(minutes=lockout_minutes)
41	
42	    db.session.commit()
43	
44	
45	def authenticate(username_or_email: str, password: str) -> Tuple[Optional[User], str]:
46	    """
47	    Returns (user, error_message). user is None on failure.
48	    """
49	    user = (
50	        User.query.filter(
51	            (User.username == username_or_email) | (User.email == username_or_email)
52	        ).first()
53	    )
54	
55	    if user is None:
56	        return None, "Invalid credentials."
57	
58	    if not user.is_active:
59	        return None, "This account has been deactivated."
60	
61	    if is_locked_out(user):
62	        minutes_left = int((user.locked_until - datetime.utcnow()).total_seconds() / 60) + 1
63	        return None, f"Account is locked. Try again in {minutes_left} minute(s)."
64	
65	    if not verify_password(password, user.password_hash):
66	        record_attempt(user, success=False)
67	        remaining = max(
68	            0,
69	            current_app.config.get("MAX_LOGIN_ATTEMPTS", 5) - user.failed_login_attempts,
70	        )
71	        if remaining == 0:
72	            return None, "Too many failed attempts. Account is now locked."
73	        return None, f"Invalid credentials. {remaining} attempt(s) remaining."
74	
75	    record_attempt(user, success=True)
76	    return user, ""

82	    """

87	        return None, "Username already taken."
88

96	        role=role,

215	def create_reservation(user_id: int, session_id: int) -> dict:
216	    """
217	    Attempt to book a spot in a studio session for a user.
218	
219	    Checks are executed in order:
220	    1. Session exists and is active.
221	    2. User does not already hold a confirmed reservation for this session.
222	    3. No time conflict with existing reservations.
223	    4. Capacity not exceeded (otherwise suggest waitlist).
224	    5. Credit score gates (blocked < 20, approval required < 50).
225	    6. Create confirmed reservation.
226	
227	    Returns
228	    -------
229	    dict
230	        On success: ``{"success": True, "reservation_id": int, "message": str}``
231	        On failure: ``{"success": False, "reason": str, "action": str}``
232	        ``action`` values: ``"none"`` | ``"waitlist"`` | ``"approval_required"``
233	        | ``"blocked"`` | ``"conflict"``
234	    """
235	    logger.info("create_reservation: user_id=%d session_id=%d", user_id, session_id)
236	
237	    # Step 1 — session exists and is active
238	    session = StudioSession.query.get(session_id)
239	    if session is None or not session.is_active:
240	        return {
241	            "success": False,
242	            "reason": "Session not found or no longer available.",
243	            "action": "none",
244	        }
245	
246	    # Step 2 — duplicate check
247	    existing = Reservation.query.filter_by(
248	        user_id=user_id, session_id=session_id, status="confirmed"
249	    ).first()
250	    if existing:
251	        return {
252	            "success": False,
253	            "reason": "You already have a reservation for this session.",
254	            "action": "none",
255	        }
256	
257	    # Step 3 — time conflict
258	    conflict = check_booking_conflicts(user_id, session_id)
259	    if conflict:
260	        cs = conflict["conflicting_session"]
261	        return {
262	            "success": False,
263	            "reason": (
264	                f"Time conflict with '{cs['title']}' at {cs['start_time']}."
265	            ),
266	            "action": "conflict",
267	        }
268	
269	    # Step 4 — capacity
270	    booked_count = Reservation.query.filter_by(
271	        session_id=session_id, status="confirmed"
272	    ).count()
273	    if booked_count >= session.capacity:
274	        return {
275	            "success": False,
276	            "reason": (
277	                f"This session is full ({booked_count}/{session.capacity} spots taken)."
278	            ),
279	            "action": "waitlist",
280	        }
281	
282	    # Step 5 — credit score gates
283	    user = User.query.get(user_id)
284	    if user.credit_score < 20:
285	        logger.warning(
286	            "create_reservation: user %d blocked (credit_score=%d)",
287	            user_id, user.credit_score,
288	        )
289	        return {
290	            "success": False,
291	            "reason": (
292	                "Your account is restricted due to low credit score. "
293	                "Please contact staff."
294	            ),
295	            "action": "blocked",
296	        }
297	
298	    if user.credit_score < 50:
299	        reservation = Reservation(
300	            user_id=user_id,
301	            session_id=session_id,
302	            status="pending_approval",
303	        )
304	        db.session.add(reservation)
305	        db.session.commit()
306	        logger.info(
307	            "create_reservation: pending_approval reservation %d created "
308	            "(user credit=%d)", reservation.id, user.credit_score,
309	        )
310	        return {
311	            "success": True,
312	            "reservation_id": reservation.id,
313	            "message": (
314	                "Your booking requires staff approval due to account standing. "
315	                "You will be notified once reviewed."
316	            ),
317	        }
318	
319	    # Step 6 — confirmed reservation
320	    reservation = Reservation(
321	        user_id=user_id,
322	        session_id=session_id,
323	        status="confirmed",
324	    )
325	    db.session.add(reservation)
326	    db.session.commit()
327	    logger.info("create_reservation: confirmed reservation %d created", reservation.id)
328	    return {
329	        "success": True,
330	        "reservation_id": reservation.id,
331	        "message": "Booking confirmed! You're all set.",
332	    }
333	
334	
335	# ── Function 4 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
336	
337	def cancel_reservation(reservation_id: int, user_id: int) -> dict:
338	    """
339	    Cancel a reservation, applying a late-cancellation breach when the session
340	    starts within 12 hours. Promotes the #1 waitlist entry if one exists.
341	
342	    Parameters
343	    ----------
344	    reservation_id : int
345	    user_id : int
346	        The requesting user. Admins may cancel any reservation; others can only
347	        cancel their own.
348	
349	    Returns
350	    -------
351	    dict
352	        ``{"success": True, "message": str, "breach": bool}``
353	        ``{"success": False, "reason": str}``
354	    """
355	    logger.info(
356	        "cancel_reservation: reservation_id=%d requested_by=%d",
357	        reservation_id, user_id,
358	    )
359	
360	    # Step 1 — load reservation
361	    reservation = Reservation.query.get(reservation_id)
362	    if reservation is None:
363	        return {"success": False, "reason": "Reservation not found."}
364	
365	    # Step 2 — ownership / admin override
366	    if reservation.user_id != user_id:
367	        requesting_user = User.query.get(user_id)
368	        if requesting_user is None or requesting_user.role != "admin":
369	            return {
370	                "success": False,
371	                "reason": "You can only cancel your own reservations.",
372	            }
373	
374	    # Step 3 — status guard
375	    if reservation.status not in ("confirmed", "pending_approval"):
376	        return {
377	            "success": False,
378	            "reason": (
379	                f"Cannot cancel a reservation with status '{reservation.status}'."
380	            ),
381	        }
382	
383	    # Step 4 — breach detection
384	    session = StudioSession.query.get(reservation.session_id)
385	    hours_until_start = (
386	        session.start_time - datetime.utcnow()
387	    ).total_seconds() / 3600
388	    breach = hours_until_start < 12
389	
390	    # Step 5 — update reservation
391	    reservation.status = "canceled"
392	    reservation.breach_flag = breach
393	    reservation.updated_at = datetime.utcnow()
394	
395	    # Step 6 — record credit penalty for breach
396	    if breach:
397	        credit_entry = CreditHistory(
398	            user_id=reservation.user_id,
399	            event_type="late_cancel",
400	            points=-1,
401	            reference_id=reservation.id,
402	            note=(
403	                f"Late cancellation for session '{session.title}' "
404	                f"({hours_until_start:.1f} hours before start)"
405	            ),
406	        )
407	        db.session.add(credit_entry)
408	        logger.warning(
409	            "cancel_reservation: breach recorded for user %d reservation %d "
410	            "(%.1fh before start)",
411	            reservation.user_id, reservation_id, hours_until_start,
412	        )
413	
414	    # Step 7 — promote waitlist

434	def reschedule_reservation(
435	    reservation_id: int, new_session_id: int, user_id: int
436	) -> dict:
437	    """
438	    Cancel an existing reservation and book a new session in one transaction.
439	
440	    The old reservation is marked ``"rescheduled"``; the new one is
441	    ``"confirmed"`` and carries ``original_reservation_id`` pointing back to the
442	    old one.
443	
444	    Parameters
445	    ----------
446	    reservation_id : int
447	        The reservation being rescheduled.
448	    new_session_id : int
449	        The target session to move to.
450	    user_id : int
451	
452	    Returns
453	    -------
454	    dict
455	        ``{"success": True, "new_reservation_id": int, "message": str, "breach": bool}``
456	        ``{"success": False, "reason": str}``
457	    """
458	    logger.info(
459	        "reschedule_reservation: reservation_id=%d new_session_id=%d user_id=%d",
460	        reservation_id, new_session_id, user_id,
461	    )
462	
463	    # Step 1 — load old reservation and verify ownership
464	    old_res = Reservation.query.get(reservation_id)
465	    if old_res is None:
466	        return {"success": False, "reason": "Reservation not found."}
467	
468	    if old_res.user_id != user_id:
469	        requesting_user = User.query.get(user_id)
470	        if requesting_user is None or requesting_user.role != "admin":
471	            return {
472	                "success": False,
473	                "reason": "You can only reschedule your own reservations.",
474	            }
475	
476	    if old_res.status not in ("confirmed", "pending_approval"):
477	        return {
478	            "success": False,
479	            "reason": (
480	                f"Cannot reschedule a reservation with status '{old_res.status}'."
481	            ),
482	        }
483	
484	    # Step 2 — validate new session capacity
485	    new_session = StudioSession.query.get(new_session_id)
486	    if new_session is None or not new_session.is_active:
487	        return {
488	            "success": False,
489	            "reason": "Target session not found or no longer available.",
490	        }
491	
492	    new_booked = Reservation.query.filter_by(
493	        session_id=new_session_id, status="confirmed"
494	    ).count()
495	    if new_booked >= new_session.capacity:
496	        return {
497	            "success": False,
498	            "reason": (
499	                "The target session is full. "
500	                "Choose another session or join its waitlist."
501	            ),
502	        }
503	
504	    # Step 3 — conflict check (excluding the reservation being rescheduled)

9	def _setup_logging(app: Flask) -> None:
10	    """Configure Python logging: console + rotating file handler."""
11	    log_dir = app.config.get("LOG_DIR", os.path.join(app.root_path, "..", "logs"))
12	    os.makedirs(log_dir, exist_ok=True)
13	    log_file = app.config.get("LOG_FILE", os.path.join(log_dir, "app.log"))
14	
15	    formatter = logging.Formatter(
16	        "[%(asctime)s] %(levelname)s in %(module)s: %(message)s",
17	        datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
18	    )
19	
20	    # Console handler
21	    console_handler = logging.StreamHandler()
22	    console_handler.setLevel(logging.DEBUG if app.debug else logging.INFO)
23	    console_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
24	
25	    # Rotating file handler (10 MB, keep 5 backups)
26	    file_handler = RotatingFileHandler(log_file, maxBytes=10 * 1024 * 1024, backupCount=5)
27	    file_handler.setLevel(logging.INFO)
28	    file_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
29	
30	    app.logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG if app.debug else logging.INFO)
31	    app.logger.addHandler(console_handler)
32	    app.logger.addHandler(file_handler)
33	
34	    # Also attach to root logger so SQLAlchemy / Werkzeug messages flow through
35	    root_logger = logging.getLogger()
36	    if not root_logger.handlers:
37	        root_logger.addHandler(console_handler)
38

55	            app.logger.info("Default admin account created (username=admin).")

59	    if config_name is None:
60	        config_name = os.environ.get("FLASK_ENV", "default")
61	
62	    app = Flask(__name__, template_folder="templates", static_folder="static")
63	    app.config.from_object(config[config_name])
64	
65	    # Ensure runtime directories exist
66	    for folder in [
67	        app.config["UPLOAD_FOLDER"],
68	        os.path.join(app.root_path, "..", "backups"),
69	        os.path.join(app.root_path, "..", "logs"),
70	    ]:
71	        os.makedirs(folder, exist_ok=True)

Unit tests for app/services/booking_service.py.
All tests use an in-memory SQLite database and call service functions directly.
"""
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from app.models.user import User
from app.models.studio import StudioSession, Reservation, Waitlist, Resource
from app.services.auth_service import hash_password
from app.services.booking_service import (
    get_sessions_for_date,
    check_booking_conflicts,
    create_reservation,
    cancel_reservation,
    reschedule_reservation,
    join_waitlist,
    promote_waitlist,
    get_user_bookings,
    leave_waitlist,
)


def test_get_sessions_for_date_returns_sessions(app, db, sample_session):
    """Verify sessions are returned for the correct date in correct format."""
    with app.app_context():
        date_str = sample_session.start_time.strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
        result = get_sessions_for_date(date_str)
        assert len(result) == 1
        assert result[0]["title"] == "Morning Yoga"
        assert result[0]["spots_remaining"] == 15
        assert "AM" in result[0]["start_time"] or "PM" in result[0]["start_time"]


def test_get_sessions_invalid_date_raises(app):
    """Verify ValueError raised for bad date format."""
    with app.app_context():
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="Invalid date format"):
            get_sessions_for_date("2026-03-31")  # wrong format


def test_create_reservation_success(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """Verify successful booking creates a confirmed reservation."""
    with app.app_context():
        result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        assert "reservation_id" in result
        reservation = Reservation.query.get(result["reservation_id"])
        assert reservation.status == "confirmed"
        assert reservation.user_id == sample_users["customer"].id


def test_create_reservation_duplicate_blocked(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """Verify user cannot double-book the same session."""
    with app.app_context():
        create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "already have a reservation" in result["reason"]


def test_conflict_detection(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Verify overlapping session times are detected."""
    with app.app_context():
        tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
        start1 = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
        session1 = StudioSession(
            title="Yoga",
            instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
            room_id=sample_room.id,
            start_time=start1,
            end_time=start1 + timedelta(hours=1),
            capacity=15,
            is_active=True,
        )
        session2 = StudioSession(
            title="Pilates",
            instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
            room_id=sample_room.id,
            start_time=start1 + timedelta(minutes=30),
            end_time=start1 + timedelta(hours=1, minutes=30),
            capacity=15,
            is_active=True,
        )
        db.session.add_all([session1, session2])
        db.session.commit()

        create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, session1.id)
        result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, session2.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert result["action"] == "conflict"


def test_capacity_full_offers_waitlist(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Verify full session returns waitlist option."""
    with app.app_context():
        tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
        start = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
        session = StudioSession(
            title="Full Class",
            instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
            room_id=sample_room.id,
            start_time=start,
            end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1),
            capacity=1,
            is_active=True,
        )
        db.session.add(session)
        db.session.commit()

API-level tests for booking HTTP endpoints.
All interactions go through the Flask test client.
"""
import pytest
from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from app.models.user import User
from app.models.studio import StudioSession, Reservation, Resource
from app.services.auth_service import hash_password


def test_schedule_page_loads(client, sample_users, login_as):
    """GET /schedule returns 200 when logged in."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get("/schedule")
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_schedule_requires_login(client):
    """GET /schedule without login redirects to login page."""
    resp = client.get("/schedule", follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 302)


def test_book_session_requires_login(client, sample_session):
    """POST /booking/reserve without login returns 401 or redirect."""
    resp = client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": sample_session.id})
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 302)


def test_book_session_success(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session):
    """POST /booking/reserve with valid data returns 201."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
    assert resp.status_code == 201


def test_book_session_duplicate_returns_error(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session):
    """POST /booking/reserve twice for the same session returns 409."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
    resp = client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
    assert resp.status_code == 409


def test_cancel_reservation_success(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session, db):
    """POST /booking/{id}/cancel returns 200."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
    reservation = Reservation.query.filter_by(user_id=sample_users["customer"].id).first()
    resp = client.post(f"/booking/{reservation.id}/cancel")
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_cancel_other_users_reservation_forbidden(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session, db):
    """POST /booking/{id}/cancel for someone else's reservation returns 403."""
    # Staff books
    from app.services.booking_service import create_reservation
    create_result = create_reservation(sample_users["staff"].id, sample_session.id)
    reservation_id = create_result["reservation_id"]

    # Customer tries to cancel it
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.post(f"/booking/{reservation_id}/cancel")
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_my_bookings_page(client, login_as, sample_users):
    """GET /booking/my-bookings returns 200 when logged in."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get("/booking/my-bookings")
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_my_bookings_requires_login(client):
    """GET /booking/my-bookings without login redirects."""
    resp = client.get("/booking/my-bookings", follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 302)


def test_my_bookings_htmx_tab_returns_fragment(client, login_as, sample_users):
    """GET /booking/my-bookings with HX-Request header returns partial HTML."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get(
        "/booking/my-bookings?tab=upcoming",
        headers={"HX-Request": "true"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    # Fragment should not contain the full page nav
    assert b"<!DOCTYPE" not in resp.data


def test_join_waitlist_success(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_room, db):
    """POST /booking/waitlist for a full session returns 201."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
    start = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
    session = StudioSession(
        title="Full",

        room_id=sample_room.id,

        capacity=0,
        is_active=True,
    )
    db.session.add(session)
    db.session.commit()
    resp = client.post("/booking/waitlist", data={"session_id": str(session.id)})
    assert resp.status_code == 201


def test_join_waitlist_requires_login(client, sample_session):
    """POST /booking/waitlist without login returns 401 or redirect."""
    resp = client.post("/booking/waitlist", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 302)


def test_session_detail_page_loads(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session):
    """GET /schedule/sessions/{id} returns 200."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get(f"/schedule/sessions/{sample_session.id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_session_detail_contains_title(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session):
    """Session detail page body includes the session title."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get(f"/schedule/sessions/{sample_session.id}")
    assert b"Morning Yoga" in resp.data


def test_session_detail_404_for_missing(client, login_as, sample_users):
    """GET /schedule/sessions/99999 returns 404."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get("/schedule/sessions/99999")
    assert resp.status_code == 404


def test_reschedule_success(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_room, db):
    """POST /booking/{id}/reschedule moves to the new session."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
    start1 = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
    start2 = tomorrow.replace(hour=14, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
    session1 = StudioSession(
        title="AM Yoga",
        instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
        room_id=sample_room.id,
        start_time=start1,
        end_time=start1 + timedelta(hours=1),
        capacity=15,
        is_active=True,
    )
    session2 = StudioSession(
        title="PM Yoga",
        instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
        room_id=sample_room.id,
        start_time=start2,
        end_time=start2 + timedelta(hours=1),
        capacity=15,
        is_active=True,
    )
    db.session.add_all([session1, session2])
    db.session.commit()

    # Book the first session
    book_resp = client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(session1.id)})
    assert book_resp.status_code == 201

    reservation = Reservation.query.filter_by(
        user_id=sample_users["customer"].id, session_id=session1.id
    ).first()

    # Reschedule to second session
    resp = client.post(
        f"/booking/{reservation.id}/reschedule",
        data={"new_session_id": str(session2.id)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert reservation.status == "rescheduled"


def test_available_sessions_returns_fragment(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session, db):
    """GET /booking/available-sessions returns HTMX fragment."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    # First book so we have a reservation_id
    from app.services.booking_service import create_reservation
    result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
    reservation_id = result["reservation_id"]

    date_str = sample_session.start_time.strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
    resp = client.get(
        f"/booking/available-sessions?reservation_id={reservation_id}&date={date_str}"
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
"}

    """POST /booking/reserve twice for the same session returns 409."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
    resp = client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
    assert resp.status_code == 409


def test_cancel_reservation_success(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session, db):
    """POST /booking/{id}/cancel returns 200."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
    reservation = Reservation.query.filter_by(user_id=sample_users["customer"].id).first()
    resp = client.post(f"/booking/{reservation.id}/cancel")
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_cancel_other_users_reservation_forbidden(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session, db):
    """POST /booking/{id}/cancel for someone else's reservation returns 403."""
    # Staff books
    from app.services.booking_service import create_reservation
    create_result = create_reservation(sample_users["staff"].id, sample_session.id)
    reservation_id = create_result["reservation_id"]

    # Customer tries to cancel it
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.post(f"/booking/{reservation_id}/cancel")
    assert resp.status_code == 403


def test_my_bookings_page(client, login_as, sample_users):
    """GET /booking/my-bookings returns 200 when logged in."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get("/booking/my-bookings")
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_my_bookings_requires_login(client):
    """GET /booking/my-bookings without login redirects."""
    resp = client.get("/booking/my-bookings", follow_redirects=False)
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 302)


def test_my_bookings_htmx_tab_returns_fragment(client, login_as, sample_users):
    """GET /booking/my-bookings with HX-Request header returns partial HTML."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get(
        "/booking/my-bookings?tab=upcoming",
        headers={"HX-Request": "true"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    # Fragment should not contain the full page nav
    assert b"<!DOCTYPE" not in resp.data


def test_join_waitlist_success(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_room, db):
    """POST /booking/waitlist for a full session returns 201."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
    start = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
    session = StudioSession(
        title="Full",
        instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
        room_id=sample_room.id,
        start_time=start,
        end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1),
        capacity=0,
        is_active=True,
    )
    db.session.add(session)
    db.session.commit()
    resp = client.post("/booking/waitlist", data={"session_id": str(session.id)})
    assert resp.status_code == 201


def test_join_waitlist_requires_login(client, sample_session):
    """POST /booking/waitlist without login returns 401 or redirect."""
    resp = client.post("/booking/waitlist", data={"session_id": str(sample_session.id)})
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 302)


def test_session_detail_page_loads(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session):
    """GET /schedule/sessions/{id} returns 200."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get(f"/schedule/sessions/{sample_session.id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 200


def test_session_detail_contains_title(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session):
    """Session detail page body includes the session title."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get(f"/schedule/sessions/{sample_session.id}")
    assert b"Morning Yoga" in resp.data


def test_session_detail_404_for_missing(client, login_as, sample_users):
    """GET /schedule/sessions/99999 returns 404."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    resp = client.get("/schedule/sessions/99999")
    assert resp.status_code == 404


def test_reschedule_success(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_room, db):
    """POST /booking/{id}/reschedule moves to the new session."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
    start1 = tomorrow.replace(hour=10, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
    start2 = tomorrow.replace(hour=14, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
    session1 = StudioSession(
        title="AM Yoga",
        instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
        room_id=sample_room.id,
        start_time=start1,
        end_time=start1 + timedelta(hours=1),
        capacity=15,
        is_active=True,
    )
    session2 = StudioSession(
        title="PM Yoga",
        instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
        room_id=sample_room.id,
        start_time=start2,
        end_time=start2 + timedelta(hours=1),
        capacity=15,
        is_active=True,
    )
    db.session.add_all([session1, session2])
    db.session.commit()

    # Book the first session
    book_resp = client.post("/booking/reserve", data={"session_id": str(session1.id)})
    assert book_resp.status_code == 201

    reservation = Reservation.query.filter_by(
        user_id=sample_users["customer"].id, session_id=session1.id
    ).first()

    # Reschedule to second session
    resp = client.post(
        f"/booking/{reservation.id}/reschedule",
        data={"new_session_id": str(session2.id)},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert reservation.status == "rescheduled"


def test_available_sessions_returns_fragment(client, login_as, sample_users, sample_session, db):
    """GET /booking/available-sessions returns HTMX fragment."""
    login_as("testcustomer", "TestPass123!")
    # First book so we have a reservation_id
    from app.services.booking_service import create_reservation
    result = create_reservation(sample_users["customer"].id, sample_session.id)
    reservation_id = result["reservation_id"]

    date_str = sample_session.start_time.strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
    resp = client.get(
        f"/booking/available-sessions?reservation_id={reservation_id}&date={date_str}"
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
"}

**77/77 tests pass** (48 pre-existing + 29 new booking tests).

Here's a summary of what was created and the one issue fixed:

**Files created:**
- `conftest.py` — updated root conftest with `db`, `sample_users`, `sample_room`, `sample_session`, `login_as` fixtures. Key fix: `set_password()` doesn't exist on the `User` model — used `password_hash=hash_password(...)` directly. The `login_as` helper posts to `identifier` (not `username`) matching the actual login form field name.
- `unit_tests/test_booking.py` — 12 tests covering all service-layer logic: date filtering, duplicate blocking, conflict detection, capacity/waitlist gate, late-cancel breach + credit deduction, waitlist promotion on cancel, reschedule linking, and both credit score gates.
- `API_tests/test_booking_api.py` — 17 tests covering HTTP layer: auth gates (unprotected endpoints return 401/302), full booking flow, cancel ownership enforcement, reschedule end-to-end, HTMX tab fragment, session detail 200/404, and waitlist join.

**Template bugs caught and fixed while writing tests:**
- `waitlist_offer.html`: `{{ session.id }}` → `{{ session_id }}` (route passes a bare int, not an ORM object)
- `waitlist_confirm.html`: `{{ result.position }}` → `{{ position }}` (route passes `position` as a standalone variable)

Staff Service Layer

```
You are continuing the StudioOps Flask + HTMX application. All booking functionality is complete. In this prompt you will ONLY create the staff business logic layer.

Create the file: app/services/staff_service.py

This file contains all staff-related business logic: check-ins, no-show handling, resource conflict detection, session CRUD, and pending approval management.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FUNCTION 1: get_staff_schedule
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

def get_staff_schedule(staff_id: int, start_date: str, end_date: str) -> list[dict]:
    """
    Get all studio sessions assigned to a staff member within a date range.

    Parameters:
        staff_id: User ID (must have role "staff")
        start_date: MM/DD/YYYY format
        end_date: MM/DD/YYYY format

    Returns list of dicts:
        {
            "session_id": int,
            "title": str,
            "date": str (MM/DD/YYYY),
            "start_time": str (12-hour),
            "end_time": str (12-hour),
            "room_name": str,
            "booked_count": int,
            "capacity": int,
            "has_resource_warning": bool,
            "is_tight_schedule": bool (True if < 15 min gap to next session)
        }
    Sorted by start_time ascending.
    """

    Implementation:
    - Parse dates. Raise ValueError if invalid format.
    - Query StudioSession where instructor_id=staff_id AND start_time between dates AND is_active=True.
    - For each session, count confirmed reservations.
    - Check resource warnings by calling check_resource_availability(session.id) — see Function 5.
    - Check tight schedule: for consecutive sessions, if gap between previous end_time and current start_time is < 15 minutes, mark both as tight.
    - Format all times in 12-hour format, dates in MM/DD/YYYY.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FUNCTION 2: get_session_roster
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

def get_session_roster(session_id: int) -> dict:
    """
    Get the roster of all confirmed reservations for a session with check-in status.

    Returns:
        {
            "session": {session details including title, date, time, room, instructor},
            "roster": [
                {
                    "reservation_id": int,
                    "customer_name": str,
                    "customer_id": int,
                    "credit_score": int,
                    "credit_color": str ("green" if >=70, "yellow" if 50-69, "red" if <50),
                    "booked_at": str (MM/DD/YYYY HH:MM AM/PM),
                    "checked_in": bool,
                    "checked_in_at": str or None (formatted 12-hour),
                    "checked_in_by": str or None (staff name),
                    "is_no_show": bool
                }
            ],
            "summary": {
                "total": int,
                "checked_in": int,
                "no_shows": int,
                "pending": int (not checked in and not no-show),
                "all_resolved": bool (True if every reservation is either checked_in or no_show)
            }
        }
    """

    Implementation:
    - Load session, raise ValueError if not found.
    - Query all Reservation where session_id AND status in ("confirmed", "completed", "no_show").
    - For each, join with User for customer info and CheckIn for check-in status.
    - credit_color: "green" if score >= 70, "yellow" if 50-69, "red" if < 50
    - Build summary counts.
    - all_resolved = (checked_in + no_shows) == total

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FUNCTION 3: perform_checkin
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

def perform_checkin(reservation_id: int, staff_id: int) -> dict:
    """
    Check in a customer for their reservation.

    Returns:
        {"success": True, "message": "Customer checked in successfully.", "checked_in_at": str}
        OR {"success": False, "reason": str}
    """

    Implementation:
    Step 1: Load reservation. If not found, return {"success": False, "reason": "Reservation not found."}.
    Step 2: If reservation.status != "confirmed", return {"success": False, "reason": f"Cannot check in a reservation with status '{reservation.status}'."}.
    Step 3: Load the session. Check if session start_time has passed (datetime.utcnow() >= session.start_time). If not, return {"success": False, "reason": "Cannot check in before the session has started."}.
    Step 4: Check if already checked in (CheckIn.query.filter_by(reservation_id=reservation_id).first()). If exists, return {"success": False, "reason": "Customer is already checked in."}.
    Step 5: Create CheckIn record:
        checkin = CheckIn(reservation_id=reservation_id, staff_id=staff_id, checked_in_at=datetime.utcnow())
        db.session.add(checkin)
    Step 6: Update reservation.status = "completed".
    Step 7: Record credit: CreditHistory(user_id=reservation.user_id, event_type="on_time", points=+2, reference_id=reservation_id, note=f"Checked in for '{session.title}'")
    Step 8: db.session.add(credit_entry)
    Step 9: db.session.commit()
    Step 10: Return {"success": True, "message": "Customer checked in successfully.", "checked_in_at": checkin.checked_in_at.strftime("%I:%M %p")}

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FUNCTION 4: mark_no_show
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

def mark_no_show(reservation_id: int, staff_id: int) -> dict:
    """
    Mark a customer as no-show after session has ended.

    Returns:
        {"success": True, "message": "Customer marked as no-show. Credit score adjusted."}
        OR {"success": False, "reason": str}
    """

    Implementation:
    Step 1: Load reservation. Verify status is "confirmed" (not already completed or no_show). If wrong status, return error.
    Step 2: Load session. Verify datetime.utcnow() >= session.end_time. If not, return {"success": False, "reason": "Cannot mark no-show until the session has ended."}.
    Step 3: Verify no CheckIn record exists for this reservation. If one exists, return {"success": False, "reason": "Customer was already checked in."}.
    Step 4: Set reservation.status = "no_show".
    Step 5: Record credit: CreditHistory(user_id=reservation.user_id, event_type="no_show", points=-3, reference_id=reservation_id, note=f"No-show for '{session.title}'")
    Step 6: db.session.commit()
    Step 7: Return success.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FUNCTION 5: check_resource_availability
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

def check_resource_availability(session_id: int) -> dict:
    """
    Check all resources for a session for overbooking or conflicts.

    Returns:
        {
            "warnings": [
                {
                    "resource_name": str,
                    "resource_type": str ("room", "instructor", "equipment"),
                    "current_usage": int,
                    "capacity": int,
                    "severity": str ("overbooked" if usage > capacity, "at_capacity" if usage == capacity, "ok")
                }
            ],
            "conflicts": [
                {
                    "type": "instructor_double_booked",
                    "instructor_name": str,
                    "conflicting_session": str (title),
                    "conflicting_time": str (formatted)
                }
            ],
            "has_issues": bool (True if any warnings with severity != "ok" OR any conflicts)
        }
    """

    Implementation:
    - Load the target session.
    - Room check: count total confirmed reservations for ALL sessions in the same room with overlapping time range. Compare against room.capacity. Time overlap: session_a.start_time < session_b.end_time AND session_b.start_time < session_a.end_time.
    - Instructor check: find other active sessions with the same instructor_id that overlap in time. If any found, add to conflicts list.
    - Equipment check: parse equipment_ids JSON from the session. For each equipment Resource, count usage across overlapping sessions that also use that equipment. Compare against equipment.capacity.
    - Build and return result dict.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FUNCTION 6: get_all_resource_warnings
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

def get_all_resource_warnings() -> list[dict]:
    """
    Get all resource warnings for upcoming sessions (next 7 days).
    Returns list of warning dicts including session info (title, date, time).
    """
    - Query all active sessions where start_time is within next 7 days.
    - For each, call check_resource_availability().
    - Collect all warnings and conflicts, enriched with session title, date (MM/DD/YYYY), time (12-hour).
    - Return flat list sorted by severity (overbooked first) then date.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FUNCTION 7: create_studio_session
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

def create_studio_session(title: str, description: str, instructor_id: int, room_id: int, start_time: datetime, end_time: datetime, capacity: int, equipment_ids: list[int] = None) -> dict:
    """
    Create a new studio session with validation.

    Returns: {"success": True, "session_id": int, "warnings": list} OR {"success": False, "reason": str}
    """

    Validations:
    - title is required and max 200 chars. If empty: return {"success": False, "reason": "Title is required."}. If too long: return {"success": False, "reason": "Title cannot exceed 200 characters."}
    - end_time must be after start_time: if not, return {"success": False, "reason": "End time must be after start time."}
    - capacity must be > 0: if not, return {"success": False, "reason": "Capacity must be at least 1."}
    - instructor must exist and have role "staff": if not, return {"success": False, "reason": "Instructor must be a staff member."}
    - room must exist and be an active Resource with type "room": if not, return {"success": False, "reason": "Room not found or inactive."}
    - Create the StudioSession record with equipment_ids serialized as JSON.
    - After creating, run check_resource_availability on the new session and include warnings in response (but don't block creation).
    - db.session.commit()
    - Return success with session_id and any warnings.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FUNCTION 8: get_pending_approvals
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

def get_pending_approvals() -> list[dict]:
    """Get all reservations with status 'pending_approval'."""
    - Query Reservation where status="pending_approval".
    - Join with User (for customer name, credit_score) and StudioSession (for session info).
    - Return list of dicts: reservation_id, customer_name, customer_id, credit_score, credit_color, session_title, session_date (MM/DD/YYYY), session_time (12-hour), booked_at.
    - Sort by booked_at ascending (oldest first).

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
FUNCTION 9: resolve_approval
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

def resolve_approval(reservation_id: int, approved: bool, staff_id: int) -> dict:
    """
    Approve or deny a pending reservation.

    Returns: {"success": True, "message": str} OR {"success": False, "reason": str}
    """
    - Load reservation. If not found, return error.
    - If reservation.status != "pending_approval", return {"success": False, "reason": "This reservation is not pending approval."}.
    - If approved:
        - Set reservation.status = "confirmed".
        - Return {"success": True, "message": f"Booking approved for {user.username}."}
    - If denied:
        - Set reservation.status = "canceled".
        - Return {"success": True, "message": f"Booking denied for {user.username}."}
    - db.session.commit()
    - Log the decision.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Do NOT create routes, templates, or tests. Only app/services/staff_service.py with all 9 functions, fully implemented with proper error handling, logging, and docstrings.
```
