            end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1),
            capacity=15,
        )
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "staff member" in result["reason"]


def test_create_session_success(app, db, sample_users, sample_room):
    """Valid inputs create a new session."""
    with app.app_context():
        tomorrow = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(days=1)
        start = tomorrow.replace(hour=14, minute=0, second=0, microsecond=0)
        result = staff_service.create_studio_session(
            title="Afternoon Pilates",
            description="Core strength class",
            instructor_id=sample_users["staff"].id,
            room_id=sample_room.id,
            start_time=start,
            end_time=start + timedelta(hours=1),
            capacity=12,
        )
        assert result["success"] is True
        assert "session_id" in result
        session = StudioSession.query.get(result["session_id"])
        assert session is not None
        assert session.title == "Afternoon Pilates"
        assert session.capacity == 12


# ── Approval tests ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_approve_pending_booking(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """Approving a pending reservation sets status to confirmed."""
    with app.app_context():
        reservation = Reservation(
            user_id=sample_users["customer"].id,
            session_id=sample_session.id,
            status="pending_approval",
        )
        db.session.add(reservation)
        db.session.commit()
        result = staff_service.resolve_approval(
            reservation.id, approved=True, staff_id=sample_users["staff"].id
        )
        assert result["success"] is True
        updated = Reservation.query.get(reservation.id)
        assert updated.status == "confirmed"


def test_deny_pending_booking(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """Denying sets status to canceled."""
    with app.app_context():
        reservation = Reservation(
            user_id=sample_users["customer"].id,
            session_id=sample_session.id,
            status="pending_approval",
        )
        db.session.add(reservation)
        db.session.commit()
        staff_service.resolve_approval(
            reservation.id, approved=False, staff_id=sample_users["staff"].id
        )
        updated = Reservation.query.get(reservation.id)
        assert updated.status == "canceled"


def test_resolve_non_pending_fails(app, db, sample_users, sample_session):
    """resolve_approval on an already-confirmed reservation returns an error."""
    with app.app_context():
        reservation = Reservation(
            user_id=sample_users["customer"].id,
            session_id=sample_session.id,
            status="confirmed",
        )
        db.session.add(reservation)
        db.session.commit()
        result = staff_service.resolve_approval(
            reservation.id, approved=True, staff_id=sample_users["staff"].id
        )
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "not pending approval" in result["reason"]


# ── Roster tests ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_get_session_roster_structure(app, db, sample_users, completed_session_with_reservation):
    """get_session_roster returns expected dict structure."""
    with app.app_context():
        data = completed_session_with_reservation
        result = staff_service.get_session_roster(data["session"].id)
        assert "session" in result
        assert "roster" in result
        assert "summary" in result
        assert len(result["roster"]) == 1
        row = result["roster"][0]
        assert row["customer_name"] == sample_users["customer"].username
        assert row["checked_in"] is False
        assert row["is_no_show"] is False
        assert result["summary"]["total"] == 1
        assert result["summary"]["pending"] == 1


def test_get_session_roster_invalid_session(app, db):
    """get_session_roster raises ValueError for unknown session_id."""
    with app.app_context():
        with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="not found"):
            staff_service.get_session_roster(99999)

session_obj = StudioSession.query.get(session_id)

-- Docs: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/how-to/capture-warnings.html
115 passed, 137 warnings in 36.51s

**115/115 passing** (77 existing + 38 new staff tests).

One service fix was required before the tests could pass: in `perform_checkin`, the existing-CheckIn guard (step 4) was moved before the status guard (step 2). After a successful check-in, the reservation status becomes `"completed"` — so calling `perform_checkin` a second time would have returned `"Cannot check in a reservation with status 'completed'"` rather than `"Customer is already checked in."` The reordering makes the duplicate-checkin check authoritative and also matches more natural semantics (the physical fact that a CheckIn record exists is checked before inspecting status fields).

**What was added:**

`conftest.py` — two new shared fixtures:
- `completed_session_with_reservation` — session started 1 hour ago, still running, with one confirmed reservation
- `ended_session_with_reservation` — session ended 30 minutes ago, with one confirmed reservation

`unit_tests/test_staff.py` — 18 unit tests across 5 groups:
- Check-in: record created, status update, credit award, pre-start guard, duplicate guard
- No-show: credit deduction, status update, pre-end guard
- Resource availability: instructor conflict detection
- Session creation: end-time validation, title required, non-staff instructor rejected, success path
- Approvals: approve→confirmed, deny→canceled, non-pending reservation rejected
- Roster: structure validation, invalid session ID raises ValueError

`API_tests/test_staff_api.py` — 20 HTTP tests covering role gates (customer→403, staff→200, admin→200), check-in/no-show endpoints, session create (201 success, 403 non-admin, 400 bad data), pending approvals page + listing + approve/deny mutations, resource warnings, resources page (admin-only), and roster page content.

FUNCTION 1: upload_file

def upload_file(file, content_id: int, upload_type: str = "cover") -> dict:
    """
    Validate and save an uploaded file.

    Parameters:
        file: werkzeug FileStorage object
        content_id: ID of the Content record this file belongs to
        upload_type: "cover" or "attachment"

    Returns:
        {"success": True, "attachment_id": int, "file_path": str, "original_filename": str}
        OR {"success": False, "reason": str}
    """

    Implementation — execute validations IN THIS ORDER:

    Step 1: Validate file exists and has a filename.
    If not file or not file.filename:
        return {"success": False, "reason": "No file selected."}

    Step 2: Extract file extension.
    ext = file.filename.rsplit(".", 1)[-1].lower() if "." in file.filename else ""

    Step 3: Validate file type.
    ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS = {"jpg", "jpeg", "png", "pdf"}
    if ext not in ALLOWED_EXTENSIONS:
        return {"success": False, "reason": f"File type '.{ext}' is not allowed. Accepted types: JPG, PNG, PDF."}

    Step 4: Read file content and check size.
    file_content = file.read()
    file_size = len(file_content)
    MAX_SIZE = 10 * 1024 * 1024  # 10 MB
    if file_size > MAX_SIZE:
        return {"success": False, "reason": f"File size ({file_size / (1024*1024):.1f} MB) exceeds the 10 MB limit."}
    file.seek(0)  # Reset for saving

    Step 5: Compute SHA-256 fingerprint.
    import hashlib
    fingerprint = hashlib.sha256(file_content).hexdigest()

    Step 6: Check for duplicate.
    existing = ContentAttachment.query.filter_by(fingerprint=fingerprint).first()
    if existing:
        return {"success": False, "reason": f"This file has already been uploaded (matches '{existing.original_filename}')."}

    Step 7: Save file.
    import uuid, os
    upload_dir = os.path.join(current_app.config["UPLOAD_FOLDER"], str(content_id))
    os.makedirs(upload_dir, exist_ok=True)
    safe_filename = f"{uuid.uuid4().hex}.{ext}"
    file_path = os.path.join(upload_dir, safe_filename)
    with open(file_path, "wb") as f:
        f.write(file_content)

    Step 8: Create ContentAttachment record.
    attachment = ContentAttachment(
        content_id=content_id,
        file_path=file_path,
        original_filename=file.filename,
        file_type=ext,
        file_size=file_size,
        fingerprint=fingerprint
    )
    db.session.add(attachment)
    db.session.commit()

    Return {"success": True, "attachment_id": attachment.id, "file_path": file_path, "original_filename": file.filename}.

def delete_file(attachment_id: int) -> dict:
    """Delete a file from disk and its database record."""
    - Load attachment by id. If not found, return {"success": False, "reason": "Attachment not found."}.
    - Try to delete file from disk using os.remove(attachment.file_path). Catch OSError and log warning.
    - Delete record from database: db.session.delete(attachment); db.session.commit()
    - Return {"success": True, "message": "File deleted."}

FUNCTION: filter_content

def filter_content(text: str) -> dict:
    """
    Run text through all active content filters.

    Returns:
        {"passed": True, "violations": []}
        OR {"passed": False, "violations": [{"pattern": str, "filter_type": str, "match": str, "position": int}]}
    """

    Implementation:
    - If text is None or empty, return {"passed": True, "violations": []}.
    - Load all ContentFilter records where is_active=True.
    - Initialize violations = [].
    - For each filter:
        If filter.filter_type == "keyword":
            index = text.lower().find(filter.pattern.lower())
            If index != -1:
                violations.append({"pattern": filter.pattern, "filter_type": "keyword", "match": text[index:index+len(filter.pattern)], "position": index})
        If filter.filter_type == "regex":
            try:
                match = re.search(filter.pattern, text, re.IGNORECASE)
                if match:
                    violations.append({"pattern": filter.pattern, "filter_type": "regex", "match": match.group(), "position": match.start()})
            except re.error as e:
                logger.warning(f"Invalid regex pattern '{filter.pattern}': {e}")
    - Return {"passed": len(violations) == 0, "violations": violations}

FUNCTION 1: get_published_content

def get_published_content(page: int = 1, per_page: int = 12, category: str = None, content_type: str = None, search: str = None, tags: list[str] = None) -> dict:
    """
    Get paginated published content with filters.

    Returns:
        {
            "items": [list of content dicts],
            "total": int,
            "page": int,
            "pages": int,
            "has_next": bool
        }

    Each content dict:
        {
            "id": int,
            "title": str,
            "content_type": str,
            "category": str,
            "tags": list[str],
            "author_name": str,
            "published_at": str (MM/DD/YYYY),
            "cover_url": str or None,
            "excerpt": str (first 200 chars of body, stripped of markdown/HTML),
            "estimated_read_time": str (e.g., "5 min read" — word_count / 200 words per minute)
        }
    """

    Implementation:
    - Start query: Content.query.filter_by(status="published")
    - If category: add filter Content.category == category
    - If content_type: add filter Content.content_type == content_type
    - If search: add filter Content.title.ilike(f"%{search}%")
    - If tags: for each tag, filter where Content.tags contains that tag (use LIKE on JSON field)
    - Order by published_at descending
    - Paginate: use .paginate(page=page, per_page=per_page)
    - For each item, build the dict. Strip HTML/markdown from body for excerpt.
    - estimated_read_time: count words in body, divide by 200, round up. Format as "N min read".
    - Return paginated result.

def get_content_detail(content_id: int, user_role: str = None) -> dict | None:
    """Get full content for display, rendering markdown to HTML if needed."""
    - Load content by id. If not found, return None.
    - If status != "published" and user_role not in ("editor", "admin"), return None.
    - If body_format == "markdown": render to HTML using the markdown library with extensions=['extra', 'codehilite', 'toc']. Use bleach or markupsafe to sanitize output.
    - If content_type == "book": load all children (chapters) where parent_id=content_id, sorted by sort_order. Build table of contents list.
    - Load all ContentAttachment records for this content.
    - Return full dict with: id, title, content_type, body_html (rendered), category, tags (parsed from JSON), author_name, published_at (MM/DD/YYYY), cover_path, attachments list, chapters list (if book), current_version.

FUNCTION 3: save_content

def save_content(content_id: int | None, data: dict, author_id: int) -> dict:
    """
    Create or update content, with version tracking and content filtering.

    data keys: title, content_type, body, body_format, category, tags (comma-separated string or list), status ("draft" or "in_review"), parent_id, sort_order

    Returns: {"success": True, "content_id": int, "version": int}
    OR: {"success": False, "reason": str, "violations": list (if filter triggered)}
    """

    Implementation:
    Step 1: Validate title.
    if not data.get("title") or not data["title"].strip():
        return {"success": False, "reason": "Title is required."}
    if len(data["title"]) > 300:
        return {"success": False, "reason": "Title cannot exceed 300 characters."}

    Step 2: Run content filter on title AND body.
    title_check = filter_content(data["title"])
    body_check = filter_content(data.get("body", ""))
    all_violations = title_check["violations"] + body_check["violations"]
    if all_violations:
        return {"success": False, "reason": "Content contains prohibited terms.", "violations": all_violations}

    Step 3: Parse tags. If tags is a string, split by comma and strip whitespace. Serialize as JSON string.
    tags = data.get("tags", [])
    if isinstance(tags, str):
        tags = [t.strip() for t in tags.split(",") if t.strip()]
    tags_json = json.dumps(tags)

    Step 4: If content_id is not None (update):
        content = Content.query.get(content_id)
        if not content:
            return {"success": False, "reason": "Content not found."}
        if content.author_id != author_id:
            author = User.query.get(author_id)
            if not author or author.role != "admin":
                return {"success": False, "reason": "You can only edit your own content."}
        # Create version snapshot BEFORE updating
        version = ContentVersion(
            content_id=content.id,
            version_number=content.current_version,
            title=content.title,
            body=content.body,
            status=content.status,
            created_by=author_id,
            change_note="Auto-saved before update"
        )
        db.session.add(version)
        # Update content fields
        content.title = data["title"]
        content.body = data.get("body", "")
        content.body_format = data.get("body_format", "markdown")
        content.content_type = data.get("content_type", content.content_type)
        content.category = data.get("category")
        content.tags = tags_json
        content.status = data.get("status", "draft")
        content.parent_id = data.get("parent_id")
        content.sort_order = data.get("sort_order", 0)
        content.current_version += 1
        content.updated_at = datetime.utcnow()

    Step 5: If content_id is None (create):
        content = Content(
            title=data["title"],
            content_type=data.get("content_type", "article"),
            body=data.get("body", ""),
            body_format=data.get("body_format", "markdown"),
            status=data.get("status", "draft"),
            author_id=author_id,
            category=data.get("category"),
            tags=tags_json,
            parent_id=data.get("parent_id"),
            sort_order=data.get("sort_order", 0),
            current_version=1
        )
        db.session.add(content)
        db.session.flush()  # Get the ID
        # Create initial version
        version = ContentVersion(
            content_id=content.id,
            version_number=1,
            title=content.title,
            body=content.body,
            status=content.status,
            created_by=author_id,
            change_note="Initial creation"
        )
        db.session.add(version)

    Step 6: db.session.commit()
    Return {"success": True, "content_id": content.id, "version": content.current_version}

def submit_for_review(content_id: int, user_id: int) -> dict:
    """Change status from 'draft' to 'in_review'. Creates a version snapshot."""
    - Load content. Verify author or admin.
    - If status != "draft", return {"success": False, "reason": "Only draft content can be submitted for review."}.
    - Create ContentVersion snapshot.
    - Set content.status = "in_review".
    - db.session.commit()
    - Return {"success": True, "message": "Content submitted for review."}

FUNCTION 5: publish_content

def publish_content(content_id: int, admin_id: int) -> dict:
    """Admin publishes content. Sets status='published', published_at, increments version."""
    - Load content. Verify admin role (load User, check role == "admin").
    - If user.role != "admin": return {"success": False, "reason": "Only admins can publish content."}
    - If content.status != "in_review": return {"success": False, "reason": "Only content in review can be published."}
    - Create ContentVersion snapshot with status="published".
    - Set content.status = "published", content.published_at = datetime.utcnow(), increment current_version.
    - db.session.commit()
    - Return {"success": True, "message": f"'{content.title}' has been published."}

def reject_content(content_id: int, admin_id: int, rejection_note: str) -> dict:
    """Admin rejects. Sets status back to 'draft'. Rejection note saved in ContentVersion."""
    - Validate admin role.
    - If not rejection_note or len(rejection_note.strip()) < 5: return {"success": False, "reason": "A rejection note is required (minimum 5 characters)."}
    - Create ContentVersion with change_note = rejection_note, status = "rejected".
    - Set content.status = "draft".
    - db.session.commit()
    - Return {"success": True, "message": "Content has been sent back to draft with feedback."}

FUNCTION 8: rollback_to_version

def rollback_to_version(content_id: int, version_id: int, user_id: int) -> dict:
    """Restore content to a previous version. Creates new version recording the rollback. Sets status to 'draft'."""
    - Load content and target version.
    - If target version doesn't belong to this content, return error.
    - Create a new ContentVersion of the CURRENT state (before rollback), with change_note = f"State before rollback to version {target.version_number}".
    - Restore content.title, content.body from target version.
    - Set content.status = "draft" (rollback always returns to draft).
    - Increment content.current_version.
    - Create another ContentVersion recording the rollback: change_note = f"Rolled back to version {target.version_number}".
    - db.session.commit()
    - Return {"success": True, "message": f"Content rolled back to version {target.version_number}. Status reset to draft."}
